Problem Statement: True or False ? If true, prove it. If false, find the true answer.
Question: The doubling time for $$y = e^{c t}$$ is $$( \text{ln} ( 2 ) ) \text{/} ( \text{ln} ( c ) ) .$$

Problem Statement: True or False ? If true, prove it. If false, find the true answer.
Question: If you invest $$\text{$} 500 ,$$ an annual rate of interest of $$3 \text{%}$$ yields more money in the first year than a $$2.5 \text{%}$$ continuous rate of interest.

Problem Statement: True or False ? If true, prove it. If false, find the true answer.
Question: If you leave a $$100 \text{°} \text{C}$$ pot of tea at room temperature $$( 25 \text{°} \text{C} )$$ and an identical pot in the refrigerator $$( 5 \text{°} \text{C} ) ,$$ with $$k = 0.02 ,$$ the tea in the refrigerator reaches a drinkable temperature $$( 70 \text{°} \text{C} )$$ more than $$5$$ minutes before the tea at room temperature.

Problem Statement: True or False ? If true, prove it. If false, find the true answer.
Question: If given a half-life of t years, the constant $$k$$ for $$y = e^{k t}$$ is calculated by $$k = \text{ln} ( 1 \text{/} 2 ) \text{/} t .$$

Problem Statement: For the following exercises, use $$y = y_{0} e^{k t} .$$
Question: If a culture of bacteria doubles in $$3$$ hours, how many hours does it take to multiply by $$10 ?$$

Problem Statement: For the following exercises, use $$y = y_{0} e^{k t} .$$
Question: If bacteria increase by a factor of $$10$$ in $$10$$ hours, how many hours does it take to increase by $$100 ?$$

Problem Statement: For the following exercises, use $$y = y_{0} e^{k t} .$$
Question: How old is a skull that contains one-fifth as much radiocarbon as a modern skull? Note that the half-life of radiocarbon is $$5730$$ years.

Problem Statement: For the following exercises, use $$y = y_{0} e^{k t} .$$
Question: If a relic contains $$90 \text{%}$$ as much radiocarbon as new material, can it have come from the time of Christ (approximately $$2000$$ years ago)? Note that the half-life of radiocarbon is $$5730$$ years.
Answer: No. The relic is approximately $$871$$ years old.

Problem Statement: For the following exercises, use $$y = y_{0} e^{k t} .$$
Question: The population of Cairo grew from $$5$$ million to $$10$$ million in $$20$$ years. Use an exponential model to find when the population was $$8$$ million.

Problem Statement: For the following exercises, use $$y = y_{0} e^{k t} .$$
Question: The populations of New York and Los Angeles are growing at $$1 \text{%}$$ and $$1.4 \text{%}$$ a year, respectively. Starting from $$8$$ million (New York) and $$6$$ million (Los Angeles), when are the populations equal?

Problem Statement: For the following exercises, use $$y = y_{0} e^{k t} .$$
Question: Suppose the value of $$\text{$} 1$$ in Japanese yen decreases at $$2 \text{%}$$ per year. Starting from $$\text{$} 1 = \text{¥} 250 ,$$ when will $$\text{$} 1 = \text{¥} 1 ?$$

Problem Statement: For the following exercises, use $$y = y_{0} e^{k t} .$$
Question: The effect of advertising decays exponentially. If $$40 \text{%}$$ of the population remembers a new product after $$3$$ days, how long will $$20 \text{%}$$ remember it?

Problem Statement: For the following exercises, use $$y = y_{0} e^{k t} .$$
Question: If $$y = 1000$$ at $$t = 3$$ and $$y = 3000$$ at $$t = 4 ,$$ what was $$y_{0}$$ at $$t = 0 ?$$

Problem Statement: For the following exercises, use $$y = y_{0} e^{k t} .$$
Question: If $$y = 100$$ at $$t = 4$$ and $$y = 10$$ at $$t = 8 ,$$ when does $$y = 1 ?$$

Problem Statement: For the following exercises, use $$y = y_{0} e^{k t} .$$
Question: If a bank offers annual interest of $$7.5 \text{%}$$ or continuous interest of $$7.25 \text{%} ,$$ which has a better annual yield?

Problem Statement: For the following exercises, use $$y = y_{0} e^{k t} .$$
Question: What continuous interest rate has the same yield as an annual rate of $$9 \text{%} ?$$

Problem Statement: For the following exercises, use $$y = y_{0} e^{k t} .$$
Question: If you deposit $$\text{$} 5000$$ at $$8 \text{%}$$ annual interest, how many years can you withdraw $$\text{$} 500$$ (starting after the first year) without running out of money?

Problem Statement: For the following exercises, use $$y = y_{0} e^{k t} .$$
Question: You are trying to save $$\text{$} 50,000$$ in $$20$$ years for college tuition for your child. If interest is a continuous $$10 \text{%} ,$$ how much do you need to invest initially?

Problem Statement: For the following exercises, use $$y = y_{0} e^{k t} .$$
Question: You are cooling a turkey that was taken out of the oven with an internal temperature of $$165 \text{°} \text{F} .$$ After $$10$$ minutes of resting the turkey in a $$70 \text{°} \text{F}$$ apartment, the temperature has reached $$155 \text{°} \text{F} \text{.}$$ What is the temperature of the turkey $$20$$ minutes after taking it out of the oven?

Problem Statement: For the following exercises, use $$y = y_{0} e^{k t} .$$
Question: You are trying to thaw some vegetables that are at a temperature of $$1 \text{°} \text{F} \text{.}$$ To thaw vegetables safely, you must put them in the refrigerator, which has an ambient temperature of $$44 \text{°} \text{F} .$$ You check on your vegetables $$2$$ hours after putting them in the refrigerator to find that they are now $$12 \text{°} \text{F} \text{.}$$ Plot the resulting temperature curve and use it to determine when the vegetables reach $$33 \text{°} \text{F} \text{.}$$

Problem Statement: For the following exercises, use $$y = y_{0} e^{k t} .$$
Question: You are an archaeologist and are given a bone that is claimed to be from a Tyrannosaurus Rex. You know these dinosaurs lived during the Cretaceous Era $$( 146$$ million years to $$65$$ million years ago), and you find by radiocarbon dating that there is $$0.000001 \text{%}$$ the amount of radiocarbon. Is this bone from the Cretaceous?

Problem Statement: For the following exercises, use $$y = y_{0} e^{k t} .$$
Question: The spent fuel of a nuclear reactor contains plutonium-239, which has a half-life of $$24,000$$ years. If $$1$$ barrel containing $$10 \text{kg}$$ of plutonium-239 is sealed, how many years must pass until only $$10 g$$ of plutonium-239 is left?

Problem Statement:
For the next set of exercises, use the following table, which features the world population by decade. \begin{table}[h]
\centering
\begin{tabular}{|c|c|}
\hline
Years since 1950 & Population (millions) \\ \hline
$$0$$ & $$2,556$$ \\ \hline
$$10$$ & $$3,039$$ \\ \hline
$$20$$ & $$3,706$$ \\ \hline
$$30$$ & $$4,453$$ \\ \hline
$$40$$ & $$5,279$$ \\ \hline
$$50$$ & $$6,083$$ \\ \hline
$$60$$ & $$6,849$$ \\ \hline
\end{tabular}
\end{table} Source : http://www.factmonster.com/ipka/A0762181.html.
Question: [T] The best-fit exponential curve to the data of the form $$P ( t ) = a e^{b t}$$ is given by $$P ( t ) = 2686 e^{0.01604 t} .$$ Use a graphing calculator to graph the data and the exponential curve together.

Problem Statement:
For the next set of exercises, use the following table, which features the world population by decade. \begin{table}[h]
\centering
\begin{tabular}{|c|c|}
\hline
Years since 1950 & Population (millions) \\ \hline
$$0$$ & $$2,556$$ \\ \hline
$$10$$ & $$3,039$$ \\ \hline
$$20$$ & $$3,706$$ \\ \hline
$$30$$ & $$4,453$$ \\ \hline
$$40$$ & $$5,279$$ \\ \hline
$$50$$ & $$6,083$$ \\ \hline
$$60$$ & $$6,849$$ \\ \hline
\end{tabular}
\end{table} Source : http://www.factmonster.com/ipka/A0762181.html.
Question: [T] Find and graph the derivative $$y^{\prime}$$ of your equation. Where is it increasing and what is the meaning of this increase?
Answer: $$P \prime ( t ) = 43 e^{0.01604 t} .$$ The population is always increasing.

Question: [T] Find the predicted date when the population reaches $$10$$ billion. Using your previous answers about the first and second derivatives, explain why exponential growth is unsuccessful in predicting the future.
Answer: The population reaches $$10$$ billion people in $$2027 .$$
